mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust economic demand, increasing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2% fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 300 fails to generate negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC > 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1 and INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 4%) increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 4% fails to generate negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC > 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1% fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS > 150k) indicate labor market strength, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS above 150k fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.
